rulings.Case Title: LEONARD D VILLAFLOR et al VS. ALFONSO R ALFONSO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disquality Attorney Rebert H. Peterson Iii
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 13, 2009, line 3. PLAINTIFFS LEONARD VILLAFLOR AND JANET VILLAFLOR'S Motion To Disqualify Attorney Robert H. Peterson III is granted. Attorney Robert H. Peterson, III is disqualified from further representation of defendant and cross-complainant pursuant of California Rules of Professional Conduct 5-210 and 3-700 due to counsel's status as a necessary material witness in the matter. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AITOR E LOYMEDY VS. VERNON LEON SANDERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Monday, July 13, 2009, Line 7, DEFENDANT ENTERPRISES RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Change Of Venue. MOTION DENIED. MOVING PAPERS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION IN THE WRONG COURT BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT SANDERS RESIDED IN SAN FRANCISCO WHEN PLAINTIFF COMMENCED THE INSTANT ACTION. MOTION DENIED BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES. FIRST, AN ANSWER SHOULD HAVE BEEN ON FILE WHEN DEFENDANT MOVED ON THAT BASIS. SEE BURON EQUIPMENT COMPANY, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665. SECOND, COUNSEL'S DECLARATIONS ARE SUBSTANTIVELY INSUFFICIENT. SEE LIEPPMAN V. LIEBER (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 914, 919. (302/CWW)
